source: Aggregated from trade-press RSS (gCaptain, Splash247, Maritime Executive, Hellenic Shipping News, OilPrice)

outages.headline: Asian Oil Importers Pivot to U.S. Supplies as Hormuz Risks Intensify
outages.summary: Asian buyers are back to negotiating spot cargoes of U.S. crude oil after the expected recovery of flows through the Strait of Hormuz ground to a halt early this week with the latest re-escalation in the U.S.-Iran tensions. Asian refiners are now returning to the trade that saved their supplies in the early days of the war—purchasing massive volumes of U.S. spot cargoes. At least three unnamed executives and traders involved in the sales and procurement of U.S. oil for Asian refiners told Bloomberg on Tuesday they had re-launched talks on…

outages.headline: China Cuts Saudi Crude Orders as Hormuz Risks and Discounts Reshape Trade
outages.summary: Some Chinese refiners have not nominated term crude cargoes from Saudi Arabia for August, while others have not been allocated any term supply for next month, as weak demand in China, competition from other producers, and continued disruptions at the Strait of Hormuz have slashed Saudi supply in recent months. At least two refiners in China have not asked for any term cargo deliveries for August, and a few others have not received provisional allocation volumes, traders with direct knowledge of the supply arrangements told Bloomberg on Tuesday.…
